Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1

I think we need to also *unset* ktpr_restrict when test-framework is done.

I know it's unlikely this will be run on a 'real' machine, but still, we're disabling a security feature and we should re-enable it
Uploaded patch set 6.

parents.subject: LU-16043 osc: allow error for write on CL_FSYNC_DISCARD

commit.subject: LU-16314 llite: Prefer %pK with seq_printf
commit.message:
LU-16314 llite: Prefer %pK with seq_printf

Update procfs and sysfs users to prefer %pK to when
printing pointers so that when kptr_restrict is set to 1
a real pointer value is provided.

To enable printing non-hashed pointer values:
  sysctl -w kernel/kptr_restrict=1

This change also sets kptr_restrict to 1 for all clients
and servers under test by test framework.

Test-Parameters: trivial
